description: The provided image displays a 'Check for updates' icon with no discernible scientific data, rendering the figure content unreadable. Based on the accompanying text extraction summary, the paper investigates Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel subjected to various heat treatments including single-stage (S1000 at 1000°C, S1050 at 1050°C, S1100 at 1100°C) and multi-stage heat treatment (MHT: 1000°C + 750°C + 1050°C). The study characterizes microstructural features through SEM, EBSD, and TEM, revealing austenite matrices with nano-scale κ-carbides (3-10 nm), Cr7C3 precipitates along grain boundaries in S1000, and B2 particles (20-160 nm) in S1050 and MHT samples. Mechanical properties reported include yield strengths ranging 827-955 MPa, ultimate tensile strengths of 998-1126 MPa, and elongations of 50.9-55.8%. Impact toughness demonstrates significant improvement in MHT (55.1 J) compared to S1000 (29.2 J), attributed to higher special grain boundary fractions and increased twin density. However, these technical details derive solely from the provided text summary rather than direct visual observation of the figure.
